Fix Notes: No fix needed; covered by post-fix regression pass (39 routes, key flows, 0 page errors).

User Story: As an admin, I want login protection with roles and per-page permissions so that users only see what they may access.
Expected Behaviour: When login protection is enabled, unauthenticated users are redirected to /login; ADMIN always has permission; other roles need route in user.permissions; users with mustChangePassword see a forced password-change screen (min 8 chars, confirm must match). In mock mode protection is disabled.
Test Result: Pass (after fix)
Errors Found: BUG-3: hasEntityVisibility() returns false when user is null, so with login protection disabled (default demo mode) 11 pages (Resources, Projects, Workload, Skills, Contracts, Rate Cards, Leaves, Requests, Interviews, Skills Map, WBS) show the permission lock screen for everyone.
Fix Notes: Fixed in AuthContext.hasEntityVisibility: returns true when login protection is disabled. Retested: all 11 previously locked pages accessible without login.

User Story: As a developer, I want the reusable DataTable component to render stable, correctly-keyed rows on desktop and mobile so that every page using it (Leaves, Resources, Projects, Contracts, Clients, Roles, Skills, Certifications, Resource Requests, Interviews, Calendar, Revenue, DB Inspector, Admin Settings, ...) is free of React reconciliation bugs.
Expected Behaviour: DataTable.tsx renders each desktop row by calling the caller-supplied renderRow(item) (which returns a <tr key={item.id}>...) and reconstructing a new <tr> around it to inject sticky/action-column styling — the reconstructed row must preserve the original key. On mobile it maps sortedData through the caller-supplied renderMobileCard(item); that array must also be reliably keyed even if a given page's renderMobileCard implementation forgets to (or intentionally returns an empty stub).
Test Result: Pass (after fix)
Errors Found: BUG-11: the desktop row-reconstruction (~line 429) spread element.props onto a freshly-created <tr> but never re-attached a key, silently losing row identity on every DataTable consumer (found via console warning on Leaves). BUG-13: a wider sweep of all 39 routes (isolated per-route, since React only logs an identical key-warning once per component name per page session and was masking repeats) found the SAME underlying warning re-surface on /admin-settings, then again on /db-inspector as each prior instance got fixed — root cause was 3 separate pages (AdminSettingsPage, DbInspectorPage, RevenuePage) passing renderMobileCard={() => <></>}, an unkeyed empty-fragment stub, to DataTable's mobile-card map.
Fix Notes: Fixed in DataTable.tsx at the component level (not per-page): the desktop reconstruction now sets key={element.key ?? rowIndex}; the mobile-card map now wraps every renderMobileCard(item) result in <React.Fragment key={item.id ?? rowIndex}>, so no current or future DataTable consumer can reintroduce this warning even with a lazy/stub mobile renderer. Also gave AdminSettingsPage.tsx a real mobile card (see AS-02) since its stub was hiding a fully-invisible mobile feature, not just a console warning. Retested: full 39-route sweep (both single-session and isolated-per-route) shows zero non-network console errors.

User Story: As a user, I want an inbox of notifications with unread filter and mark-as-read so that I don't miss approvals.
Expected Behaviour: Default filter 'Da Leggere'; unread count shown in page header, sidebar nav badge and header bell (HeaderActions), all sourced from the same UIConfigContext.notifications list; clicking a notification marks it read and navigates to its link; 'Segna tutte come lette' bulk action zeroes unread count everywhere and persists after reload; icons inferred from title keywords (Richiesta/Assegnazione/Allocazione, case-insensitive) with 'info' fallback; relative dates (time/Ieri/date); empty state text differs for 'Tutte' vs 'Da Leggere'.
Test Result: Pass (after fix)
Errors Found: BUG-5 (prior): fetchNotifications() was never called — fixed previously. This pass found 3 new defects via live Playwright testing of the leave-request notification flow: BUG-8: LeavePage.tsx created the notification link as '/#/leaves' (HashRouter fragment baked into the value) instead of a plain path; passing this to react-router's navigate() resolved to pathname '/' and silently redirected to the role's default home (/staffing) instead of /leaves — verified live (URL landed on /#/staffing, not /#/leaves). BUG-9: the icon-inference check notification.title.includes('Richiesta') is case-sensitive, but LeavePage.tsx created the title as 'Nuova richiesta di assenza' (lowercase r) — the single most common real-world notification always showed the generic 'info' icon instead of 'assignment'. BUG-10: the empty-state paragraph rendered 'Non ci sono notifiche .' (stray double space + orphaned period) when the 'Tutte' filter has zero results, because the space sat outside the conditional string.
Fix Notes: Fixed in LeavePage.tsx: link now '/leaves' and title now 'Nuova Richiesta di Assenza' (matches the Title-Case convention used by every other notification title in api/resources.ts and api/staffing.ts). Fixed in NotificationsPage.tsx: icon inference now lower-cases the title before matching (defends against future casing drift) and the empty-state string no longer produces a stray space. Retested end-to-end: creating a leave request now shows the 'assignment' icon and clicking it navigates to /leaves (verified via URL assertion); 'Tutte' empty state reads 'Non ci sono notifiche.' cleanly.

User Story: As an admin, I want to bulk edit talent flags and seniority codes so that grading is fast, on both desktop and mobile.
Expected Behaviour: Searchable table of active resources with inline checkbox and code input; changes staged locally and saved in one bulk action with toast; DataTable shows a desktop table (>=md) and equivalent per-resource cards on mobile (<md), reusing the same column cell renderers so edits work identically on both.
Test Result: Pass (after fix)
Errors Found: BUG-12: AdminSettingsPage.tsx passed renderMobileCard={() => <></>} to DataTable — every row rendered a blank, keyless fragment on mobile. Net effect: below the md breakpoint the desktop table is hidden (DataTable.tsx's own 'hidden md:block' wrapper) and this mobile stub rendered nothing, so the entire bulk talent/seniority editor was invisible and unusable on phones, with no message explaining why. It also logged 'Each child in a list should have a unique key prop... Check the render method of DataTable' since React saw multiple identical keyless fragments in the array.
Fix Notes: Fixed in AdminSettingsPage.tsx: renderMobileCard now returns a real per-resource card (key={r.id}) that reuses the same columns[].cell(r) renderers as the desktop table, so the talent checkbox and seniority input are editable on mobile too. Retested at 390px viewport: card renders with 'RISORSA' field label, no console key-prop warning.
